title: Multiplying a Multiple of 10 by a Single Digit

steps.explanation: 40 is 4 tens. We can multiply the non-zero part first.
Ignore the zero for now and multiply the basic fact.
40 has one trailing zero, so the product gets one trailing zero.
Strategy: multiply the basic fact, then attach the trailing zeros.
Both numbers are multiples of 10. We multiply the basic facts and attach all the trailing zeros.
Strip the zeros and multiply the leading digits.

steps.instruction: How many trailing zeros are there in 30 and 40 combined?
steps.work: 30 has 1 zero, 40 has 1 zero → 2 zeros total.
steps.explanation: Add up the trailing zeros from both factors.

steps.instruction: Put the zeros on the product.

steps.explanation: This works for any multiples of 10, 100, or 1,000.
